predictions.reasoning: NIS2 Art. 21 explicitly requires risk management measures based on an all-hazards approach to control risks to network and information systems; this directly aligns with the Cyber Risk Management Process identifying, assessing and treating such risks.

The regulation mandates appropriate technical, operative and organisational measures for cybersecurity risk management; ISMS governs security policies and measures across the IT landscape, directly supporting this requirement.

NIS2 Art. 21 requires technical measures to protect network and information systems from security incidents; encryption is a fundamental technical control supporting system security and incident prevention.
